subject: Geography
title: The Arctic Border Rush
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Arctic Border Rush.

hook.text: Five nations claim territory in the Arctic. As ice melts, the stakes are rising.

buildup.text: Russia, Canada, the US, Norway, and Denmark all want the Arctic's oil and shipping lanes.

discovery.text: In 2007, Russia planted a flag on the seabed under the North Pole—a symbolic land grab.

twist.text: Canada responded by naming a new Arctic patrol ship and mapping its continental shelf.

climax.text: Climate change is creating borders where ice once made the question irrelevant.

punchline.text: Melting ice is the world's newest border dispute.

quiz.question: Why is the Arctic becoming a border dispute?
options: Melting ice reveals resources and shipping lanes
